content.text:
The zoomed view clearly shows atom **C:18** and all its covalent bonds. I can see three bonds extending from C:18:

1. A bond to **C:19** (upper-left)
2. A bond to **C:17** (right)
3. A bond to **H:22** (lower-left)

Here are the results:

## Neighbour Atoms of Atom 18 (C)

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 19 | C |
| 17 | C |
| 22 | H |

## Molecule Information
- **geomconf_index**: **8154** (from the filename `geomconf_8154.xyz`)

Atom C:18 is part of a five-membered thiophene ring (containing S:20), and it is bonded to two adjacent ring carbons (C:17 and C:19) and one hydrogen (H:22).

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 18, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
